data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was caused by a systemic failure of the palatial redistribution economy due to seismic disruption of trade routes and agricultural surpluses.", "mechanism": "A cluster of high-magnitude earthquakes (earthquake storm) physically destroyed key palatial centers and disrupted the fragile, centralized logistics network required to move grain and bronze, leading to famine and loss of military capacity.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "Cyprus", "Mesopotamia"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological layers at Pylos or Hattusa showing no evidence of seismic damage prior to destruction by fire.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "This aligns with the 'Earthquake Storm' hypothesis, explaining the simultaneity across fault lines while excluding Egypt which lies on a different tectonic plate."}
